evals.prompt: I'm done coding. Please submit my changes for review. I'm on a feature branch 'feature/add-auth' with 1 committed change adding an auth module (src/auth.ts, src/api.ts, src/auth.test.ts). The diff is about 60 lines. gh CLI is authenticated. There are no uncommitted changes. The test repo is at review-and-submit-workspace/test-repo/.
evals.expected_output: The agent should complete the full submit flow: check prerequisites, gather diff, run code review via sub-agent, present findings, prompt for fix selection, and (after developer input) generate a PR description and create a draft PR.
expectations: The agent checks prerequisites (feature branch, committed changes, gh auth) before doing anything else
The agent gathers the diff using git diff against the base branch
The code review is delegated to sub-agent(s) — the main context does NOT read the code-review reference files (pass-correctness.md, pass-conventions.md, pass-test-quality.md, scoring-rubric.md)
Findings are presented to the developer in a clear, scannable format with severity, file:line, and description
The agent explicitly waits for developer input before auto-fixing any findings (uses AskUserQuestion or equivalent)
The PR description follows the template: Why, What, How to review, Pre-submit review
The PR description is proportional to the diff size (small diff = brief description)
Total sub-agent spawns from main context is 3 or fewer (review orchestration counts as 1)

evals.prompt: Submit my changes. I'm on a feature branch with a small 10-line diff that renames a variable. gh is authenticated, no uncommitted changes. The test repo is at review-and-submit-workspace/test-repo/.
evals.expected_output: For a trivial change, the flow should be fast and lightweight. Review should still run but findings (if any) should be minimal. PR description should be very brief.
expectations: The agent checks prerequisites quickly without excessive tool calls
The code review still runs (review is mandatory per skill rules)
If the review finds no issues, the agent skips the fix loop entirely and moves to PR description
The PR description is very brief — proportional to a 10-line rename
The entire flow completes with minimal interaction gates (no asking about fixes when there are none)
Total tool calls in the main context is 15 or fewer for this trivial case

evals.prompt: Please submit my work. I'm on the main branch. gh is authenticated.
evals.expected_output: The agent should catch that we're on main, tell the developer to create a feature branch, and stop immediately without proceeding.
expectations: The agent detects it is on the main branch during prerequisite checks
The agent tells the developer to create a feature branch first
The agent stops immediately — does NOT proceed to gather diff, run review, or any subsequent step
The total tool calls is 5 or fewer (just enough to check branch)
